name: realtime
description: The WebSocket transport substrate — wraps Laravel Reverb behind attribute-driven channel registration. Ships THREE owned entities: `BroadcastChannel` (registered namespace + type IN public/private/presence + authorization_callback FQCN — discovered at boot via `#[AsBroadcastChannel]`), `BroadcastSubscription` (active WS subscription of a User to a channel; state machine pending_auth → active → disconnected; auto-purge on disconnected+last_seen>15m), `Presence` (ephemeral Redis-backed row per (channel, user) tracking who's online with periodic snapshot). Every tenant broadcasts on `tenant.{id}.*` prefix — cross-tenant channel names refused at broadcast time by TenantBoundaryEnforcer middleware. Publish contract: domain modules emit typed events, the compiler discovers `implements ShouldBroadcast` at boot, and each event auto-registers on its declared channel. Consumer contract: frontend `useSubscribe(channelName, handler)` hooks in the `@stackra/realtime` package handle authentication + reconnect + presence roster. Auth flow: sanctum token exchanged for Reverb channel signed URL at `POST /broadcasting/auth` (bundled). NOT a message bus — for ephemeral live updates only (RSVP counts, live attendance, standings). Durable messaging → notifications; audit trail → activity/audit.

attribute_driven.description: Domain modules register broadcast channels via `#[AsBroadcastChannel]` on a class or method. Boot-time compiler adds the channel + its authorization callback to `BroadcastChannelRegistry`.

attributes.AsBroadcastChannel: Marks a class as a channel registrar. `#[AsBroadcastChannel(namespace: 'tenant.{tenantId}.attendance', type: 'private', authorize: [AttendanceChannelAuth::class, 'authorize'])]`. Params: namespace (Laravel channel string with placeholders), type (public/private/presence), authorize (callable FQCN + method resolving to bool).

tenant.description: Broadcast auth endpoint + presence roster read.
